title: Viking Therapeutics Announces Positive Phase 2 Results for Oral VK2735
text: Viking Therapeutics reported encouraging data from its Phase 2 VENTURE-Oral Dosing trial evaluating VK2735, a dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist tablet formulation for obesity treatment. The study achieved its primary and secondary endpoints, demonstrating significant weight reduction. Participants receiving the highest dose experienced up to 12.2% (26.6 lbs) mean weight loss compared to just 1.3% for placebo. Across treatment groups, up to 97% of subjects achieved at least 5% weight loss versus 10% in the placebo arm. Up to 80% of VK2735-treated participants achieved at least 10% weight loss, compared to 5% on placebo. The medication proved generally well-tolerated with approximately 98% of reported drug-related treatment-emergent adverse events categorized as mild or moderate. Gastrointestinal side effects like nausea occurred in 58% of treated participants versus 48% for placebo, with 99% of nausea characterized as mild or moderate. An exploratory cohort tested transitioning patients from 90mg daily doses down to 30mg, and results suggested weight loss benefits persisted at lower maintenance doses, supporting potential long-term treatment strategies. CEO Brian Lian noted the progressive nature of the weight loss curves suggests potential for further improvement with longer dosing periods.
